content:
{
  "title": "Syntax-Aware Scaffolding Prompts for Enhanced Code Generation in LLMs",
  "problem": "Existing code generation prompts often neglect to guide LLMs on code structure, leading to syntactic errors and inconsistencies. There is a need for prompting methods that better steer LLMs toward syntactically correct and well-structured code.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly deployed for code generation tasks, their output quality becomes critical for developer productivity and trust. By incorporating explicit syntax and structure guidance into prompts, we can potentially mitigate common failure modes such as missing blocks, wrong indentation, or incomplete implementations. Recent advances in prompt engineering suggest that more structured prompts can unlock new capabilities in LLMs.",
  "method": "We propose Syntax-Aware Scaffolding Prompts (SASP), which augment standard code generation prompts with lightweight, language-specific scaffolds that explicitly specify function signatures, indentation levels, required control structures, and placeholders for logic. Prompts are automatically constructed by parsing problem descriptions and injecting scaffold code with comments or TODOs indicating required steps (e.g., looping, error handling). The LLM is then prompted to 'fill in' the scaffold, leveraging syntax cues while retaining creative flexibility. SASP can be adapted to multiple programming languages by utilizing language grammars to generate appropriate scaffolding.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate SASP on HumanEval, MBPP, and CodeContests benchmarks across Python, JavaScript, and Java. Baselines include vanilla natural language prompts and retrieval-augmented few-shot exemplars. Metrics will comprise functional correctness (pass@k), syntactic correctness, and code readability (automated and human-rated). Ablation studies will assess the effect of different scaffold components."
}
